member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.HangfireExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods on for Hangfire integration.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.HangfireExtensions.WithHangfireJobs(AspNetCore.Multitenancy.MultitenancyBuilder)
member.summary:
Registers tenant-aware Hangfire services:
— propagates tenant context into jobs
— resolves jobs from per-tenant DI scope
— schedules jobs per tenant
You still need to call services.AddHangfire(...) and services.AddHangfireServer() separately with your storage configuration (SQL Server, Redis, etc.).

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.HangfireExtensions.UseTenantJobFilter(Hangfire.IGlobalConfiguration,System.IServiceProvider)
T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantBackgroundService
member.summary: Base class for tenant-aware hosted services (). Override to implement per-tenant logic. The base class iterates all active tenants and calls your method in a scoped tenant context.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantBackgroundService.ExecuteForTenantAsync(AspNetCore.Multitenancy.ITenantContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Override to implement per-tenant background work. Called once per active tenant on each execution cycle.
member.param: The tenant context for the current tenant.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantBackgroundService.DelayAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Override to control how long to wait between execution cycles. Default: 1 minute.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantJobActivator
member.summary: Resolves Hangfire job instances from a per-tenant DI scope, ensuring that any scoped services (including ) are resolved from the correct tenant scope.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantJobFilter
member.summary:
Hangfire server filter that automatically propagates tenant context into background jobs.
On enqueue: serializes the current TenantId into the Hangfire job parameters. On execute: reads TenantId from job params, creates a tenant DI scope, sets , then executes the job in that scope.
Register once in Program.cs — jobs see a fully populated ITenantContext identical to an HTTP request context.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantJobFilter.OnCreating(Hangfire.Client.CreatingContext)
member.summary: Serializes the current tenant ID into the Hangfire job parameters at enqueue time.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantJobFilter.OnPerforming(Hangfire.Server.PerformingContext)
member.summary: Restores the tenant context before executing the job.

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantJobFilter.OnPerformed(Hangfire.Server.PerformedContext)
member.summary: Disposes the tenant scope after job execution.

member.name: T:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantRecurringJobManager
member.summary: Schedules Hangfire recurring jobs for every active tenant automatically. Use this instead of directly to ensure jobs run once per tenant with a properly populated .

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantRecurringJobManager.ScheduleForAllAsync``1(System.String,System.String,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Threading.Tasks.Task}},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Schedules as a recurring Hangfire job for every active tenant. Each tenant gets its own job entry: {jobId}-{tenantId}.
member.typeparam: The Hangfire job type. Must have an Execute() or ExecuteAsync() method.
member.param: Expression pointing to the job method. Example: job => job.ExecuteAsync()

member.name: M:AspNetCore.Multitenancy.BackgroundJobs.TenantRecurringJobManager.RemoveForAllAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Removes all recurring job entries for the given job ID across all tenants.
